faqs.question: Why do valves and seals need regular checking?
faqs.answer: Valves and seals wear out over time due to constant water pressure, mineral buildup, and age. Regular checks catch deterioration early, preventing slow leaks that can cause water damage and mold growth.

faqs.question: What types of valves are inspected?
faqs.answer: We inspect all accessible valves including shut-off valves, supply valves under sinks and toilets, the main water shut-off, hose bibs, and washing machine valves. We also check all rubber seals and gaskets.

faqs.question: How do I know if a valve or seal is failing?
faqs.answer: Signs include dripping around valve handles, difficulty turning valves on or off, water stains near fixtures, and small puddles under sinks or behind toilets. If you notice any of these, schedule an inspection.
